commands.review_summary:
The PR changes core `/status` and bundled Codex `/codex status` formatters and tests to emit GFM hard-break markers between intentional status rows, including embedded multiline details.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +8. Total +15 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main builds both status outputs with lone `\n` rows and Telegram rich delivery sends Markdown as rich Markdown without status-specific hard-break conversion. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the maintainer-labeled draft PR mainly needs the pending Telegram proof or maintainer landing decision.

target.title: fix(status): preserve rich message line breaks
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve intentional row layout in core `/status` and plugin `/codex status` when Telegram sends Bot API 10.1 Rich Markdown
- emit standard GFM hard line breaks for status rows, including embedded multiline status details
- keep the generic Telegram rich renderer unchanged so arbitrary Markdown retains normal GFM soft-break semantics

## Root cause

Telegram Rich Markdown is GFM-compatible. In GFM, a lone newline is a soft break and a conforming renderer may display it as a space; two trailing spaces before the newline create an explicit hard break. These status commands produce layout rows rather than prose, so the producer should declare those breaks instead of globally rewriting every rich Markdown newline.

Release note: Fixed Telegram `/status` and `/codex status` messages losing their intended line breaks when sent as rich messages.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts` (203 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/status/status-message.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts extensions/codex/src/command-formatters.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/status/status-message.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts extensions/codex/src/command-formatters.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- fresh local autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- remote `check:changed`: blocked by local infrastructure: `blacksmith` executable missing; Azure CLI session expired; AWS Crabbox broker not configured
- Mantis Telegram live and desktop proof: pending after PR creation

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram rich status messages collapse intentional status rows into one paragraph.
Real environment tested: Pending Mantis Telegram live and desktop proof on this PR head.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Run `/status` and `/codex status` through the Telegram rich-message delivery path.
Evidence after fix: Pending Mantis workflow artifacts and PR evidence comment.
Observed result after fix: Focused tests confirm every intentional status row, including embedded multiline details, emits a GFM hard break.
What was not tested: Non-Telegram visual rendering; the hard-break markers are invisible to plain-text consumers.
